comment: Phase 30c: federation seed list, baked into every node binary.
Each entry is a peer the bundled distribution KNOWS will accept
/info reads (HTTP) and gossip (QUIC) at boot. The list is
intentionally append-only — never remove a seed without bumping
the major version, since older nodes still trust it.
An installed node may shadow this list by writing its own copy
to /data/l1/seeds.json (see morm_l1/seed_loader.py). Normal
operation: the loader merges baked-in + user-overridden + the
live `/info.peers` discovered at runtime, dedups, and feeds the
result to --peers if the operator left it empty.
We deliberately do not depend on a single-DNS-zone bootstrap
(3原則: no LLC, no privileged registry). The discovery section
below lists multiple parallel update channels — at least one
must be reachable for a fresh node to obtain a current list.

DNS SRV discovery is opt-in. The previous default '_morm-seeds._tcp.morm.network' has been removed because the morm.network domain is not currently registered by this project, and a third-party registration could publish hostile peer URLs that nodes with dnspython installed would automatically consume. Operators who want DNS-driven discovery must set this to a domain THEY control.
Operator may set github_raw_seeds_url to e.g.
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<gh-user>/morm/main/morm-l1/morm_l1/seeds.json
and the loader will fetch it on a 24h cadence to learn about
newer peers without a software upgrade. ipfs_seed_cid likewise
may pin a CID that publishes the same JSON.
